title: AI & Management Consulting Intern (Value Engineering - DACH Market)
description:
## The Team
As an Intern in AI & Management Consulting you join our Value Engineering team to focus on driving and closing opportunities by identifying and selling the value Celonis can bring to customers. Our Value Engineers (VE) are the trusted advisor for customers and work on the intersection of technology and business. Value Engineers partner with sales and customers to create compelling solutions to critical business problems.

## The Role
Value Engineers are the trusted advisors for our prospects and customers and leverage product expertise and business acumen to solve our prospects’ and customers’ most pressing challenges. You will gain experience in executing proof of value projects and take part in technical discussions with stakeholders. By creating customer-based business cases, you support the strategic business development activities for the DACH market.

## The work you’ll do
- Analyze data from our customers to increase transparency in business processes
- Understand the pain points of our customers and translate them into actionable use cases in the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform
- Collaborate with customers and partners to uncover their core business challenges and determine how Celonis Process Intelligence can help them achieve their business objectives
- Quantify financial impact of use cases implemented in the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform and prepare management ready presentations
- Present findings together with your team in front of customers’ C-level and decision makers
- Leverage Celonis data modeling and AI capabilities to uncover process inefficiencies, generate insights, and deliver high impact, data driven value propositions to clients.
- Pitch the Celonis AI value proposition and vision to customers and align our solutions with their AI strategy
- Build strong relationships with our customers by winning their trust through technical, product expertise, and business acumen

## The qualifications you need
- Student of Business Informatics / Management & Technology / Industrial Engineering/ Computer Science/ Economics / Mathematics or of a comparable program
- Strong communicational skills and interest in interacting with potential customers
- Confidence to present to C-level executives and larger audiences
- Have a passion for technology, data, OpenAI and LLMs and the impact it can have on the world
- Experience in consulting and highly interested in Data Analytics & Applied AI
- Knowledge in SQL and Python or related database query languages (completion of Celonis "Rising Star" modules would be a plus)
- Analytical mindset and the ability to multi-task, prioritize and execute
- Effective communicator and **business fluent in German and English**
- **Full-time availability** for at least **five months** starting from **June 2026**

## What Celonis Can Offer You
- **Pioneer Innovation:** Work with the leading, award-winning process mining technology, shaping the future of business.
- **Accelerate Your Growth:** Benefit from clear career paths, internal mobility, a dedicated learning program, and mentorship opportunities.
- **Receive Exceptional Benefits:** Including generous PTO, hybrid working options, company equity (RSUs), comprehensive benefits, extensive parental leave, dedicated volunteer days, and much more.
- **Prioritize Your Well-being:** Access to resources such as gym subsidies, counseling, and well-being programs.
- **Connect and Belong:** Find community and support through dedicated inclusion and belonging programs.
- **Make Meaningful Impact:** Be part of a company driven by strong values: Live for Customer Value, The Best Team Wins, We Own It, and Earth Is Our Future.
- **Collaborate Globally:** Join a dynamic, international team of talented individuals.
- **Empowered Environment:** Contribute your ideas in an open culture with autonomous teams.
summary: Celonis is seeking an AI & Management Consulting Intern for its Value Engineering team focusing on the DACH market. The role involves analyzing customer data, creating business cases, and leveraging AI and process intelligence to drive value for C-level stakeholders. Candidates must be students in technical or business fields, fluent in German and English, and available full-time for at least five months starting June 2026.

title: Client Engagement Partner - Manufacturing / Automotive
description:
## The Role

The Client Value Partner (CVP) role is a key addition to our broader Value Engineering team and will play a crucial role in Celonis’ growth strategy. As a CVP, you will spearhead our mission of data-driven business transformation with our most strategic customers within the **Manufacturing/Automotive** industry. You will lead extensive teams of Celonis experts to ensure the success and satisfaction of some of our top customer base on their journey to realizing value with Celonis.

The CVP is the accountable lead for customer success, acting as the trusted advisor for a strategic customer. This involves engaging with C-Level Executives to collaboratively define the long-term vision for utilizing Celonis to transform their business while mobilizing the necessary Celonis resources. In collaboration with our cross-functional Celonis teams, you will drive measurable business impact across various processes and industries and accelerate the adoption of our platform. The CVP will also champion transformational initiatives across Celonis and customer accounts, fostering innovation and strategic change to maximize value and drive business transformation.

## The work you’ll do

### Accountable Program Lead & Engagement Manager
- Develop and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders, including C-level internal / external executives, embedding Celonis into their strategic objectives.
- Ensure engagements are properly staffed leveraging resources from global cross-functional Celonis teams, such as Servicing, Product & Engineering
- Mentor customer on organization design and maturation of Centers of Excellence to focus on increasing adoption of our platform.
- Mentor and coach team members, providing guidance and support for their success, and offer feedback to managers on team performance.

### Driving Customer Value
- Responsible for the end-to-end customer value journey
- Influence key customer stakeholders through executive communication, product knowledge, and industry/domain expertise.
- Work with senior stakeholders to establish the Celonis program roadmap and detailed value plans.
- Resolve customer escalations orchestrating internal teams efficiently and effectively in a timely manner
- Internalize the customer's strategy and targets and drive Celonis delivery alignment to that strategy, ensuring that we support the customer in solving business-critical problems
- Drive value realization and adoption within accounts, ensuring high-quality, on-time project completion, client satisfaction, and meeting usage and adoption targets.

### Community Builder and Customer Advocacy
- Organize and lead customer advisory boards to address industry-specific challenges and innovations, establishing a structured feedback loop to continuously gather insights and improve products and services.
- Act as the voice of the customer within the company, ensuring alignment of product development and service offerings with industry needs and customer feedback, while collaborating with the customer advocacy team on advocacy strategies.
- Drive customer presence and sponsorship at key Celonis and industry events, serving as a Celonis leader at conferences, webinars, and through published thought leadership.
- Collaborate on innovation or new asset development in specific domains, and contribute to playbooks, resources, and tools for the value engineering team to enhance their effectiveness.

## The Qualifications you need
- Minimum 10 years of relevant customer success, strategy consulting, or related experience within the Manufacturing/Automotive required
- Strong experience in driving large scale transformation programs, process re-engineering engagements, and implementations
- Experience with AI-based transformation is a strong plus
- Deep exposure to and experience with successful change management practices
- Ability to navigate large global organizations and have the power of persuasion through data, facts and confidence
- Knows how to successfully work with internal resources to design, pitch and sell opportunities with relevant subject matter experts and practice leads
- Possess a natural and consultative approach when engaging with senior executives
- Strong team player who inspires the team with a patient and entrepreneurial leadership style
- Project management: ability to organize and manage multiple streams
- Strong communication skills and ability to translate Celonis use cases into customer business landscape
- Are self-motivated, proactive team player
- Are able to thrive in fast-paced, collaborative, and ambiguous environments with various stakeholders

## What Celonis Can Offer You
- **Pioneer Innovation:** Work with the leading, award-winning process mining technology, shaping the future of business.
- **Accelerate Your Growth:** Benefit from clear career paths, internal mobility, a dedicated learning program, and mentorship opportunities.
- **Receive Exceptional Benefits:** Including generous PTO, hybrid working options, company equity (RSUs), comprehensive benefits, extensive parental leave, dedicated volunteer days, and much more.
- **Prioritize Your Well-being:** Access to resources such as gym subsidies, counseling, and well-being programs.
- **Connect and Belong:** Find community and support through dedicated inclusion and belonging programs.
- **Make Meaningful Impact:** Be part of a company driven by strong values that guide everything we do: Live for Customer Value, The Best Team Wins, We Own It, and Earth Is Our Future.
- **Collaborate Globally:** Join a dynamic, international team of talented individuals.
- **Empowered Environment:** Contribute your ideas in an open culture with autonomous teams.
summary: Celonis is seeking a Client Engagement Partner (Client Value Partner) for the Manufacturing/Automotive industry. This high-level role acts as a trusted advisor to C-level executives, leading data-driven business transformations and managing the end-to-end customer value journey using Celonis' Process Intelligence technology.

title: Research Engineer for AI Validation (f/m/div.)
description:
## Company Overview
**At Bosch**, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: We grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Welcome to Bosch.

## Role Overview
As a **Research Engineer for AI Validation**, you will develop innovative methods for the validation and release of complex AI models, including probabilistic, generative, and large-scale models.

## What You Will Do
- Develop innovative methods for the **validation and release of complex AI models** (probabilistic, generative, and large-scale models).
- Design and develop forward-looking concepts in close collaboration with validation teams in corporate research and business units.
- Analyze technological requirements, particularly regarding **validation and safety of time-series-processing models**, in coordination with business units.
- Implement and engineer **prototypical software solutions** and demonstrate them in concrete Bosch use cases.
- Integrate existing technologies into toolboxes that support the validation of modern AI functions.

## Requirements

### Education
- Completed **PhD in Computer Science, Statistics, Physics**, or a comparable technical field.

### Experience and Knowledge
- **Several years of experience** in AI-related topics within industrial product development (e.g., consumer goods, automotive, manufacturing).
- Hands-on involvement in **product release processes, safety standards, and validation methods** in software and AI environments.
- Proven expertise in **modern AI algorithms** and principles: 
    - Generative and large-scale models
    - Deep neural networks
    - Foundation models
    - Agentic AI
    - Strong background in **statistics**
- Extensive experience in developing **robust, safe, and reliable AI solutions**, including the integration of AI methods with formal methods and statistical approaches.
- Strong skills in implementing AI algorithms in **Python**, applying modern, production-grade software development practices and standards.

### Personality and Working Practice
- Ability to communicate complex technical topics clearly.
- Ability to work successfully in interdisciplinary and international teams.

### Languages
- **English**: Business fluent (written and spoken).
- **German**: A plus.

## Benefits
- Flexible working models: various part-time options, mobile working, and job sharing.
- A corporate culture anchored in diversity and inclusion.

## Additional Information
- Links: [bosch-ai.com](https://www.bosch-ai.com), [bosch.com/research](https://www.bosch.com/research)
- Application documents required: CV, letter of motivation, certificates, and links to GitHub or Kaggle accounts.
summary: Bosch is seeking a Research Engineer with a PhD to develop validation methods for complex AI models, including generative and large-scale models. The role focuses on creating robust and safe AI solutions for industrial product development, requiring a strong background in Python, statistics, and software engineering.

title: Internship Machine Learning & Predictive Analytics for Heat Pump
description:

Linnan Du (Functional Department)
+49 7153 3068232

title: Product Manager*in (m/w/div.) Climate & Comfort
description:
## Company Overview
**Bei Bosch gestalten wir Zukunft mit hochwertigen Technologien und Dienstleistungen, die Begeisterung wecken und das Leben der Menschen verbessern.** Unser Versprechen an unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter steht dabei felsenfest: Wir wachsen gemeinsam, haben Freude an unserer Arbeit und inspirieren uns gegenseitig.

**Willkommen bei Bosch.**

Die Robert Bosch Smart Home GmbH wurde im Jahr 2016 als 100-prozentige Tochtergesellschaft der Robert Bosch GmbH gegründet. In agilen Teams mit offener Arbeitskultur, flachen Hierarchien und inspirierender Arbeitsumgebung entwickeln wir intelligent vernetzte Geräte, Features und Services. Unser Fokus ist es, Nutzern begeisternde Produkte für ein komfortables und sicheres Zuhause zu bieten.

## Role Overview
As a **Product Manager*in (m/w/div.) Climate & Comfort**, you will take responsibility for products in the Climate & Comfort area of the Smart Home system, managing the complete product and feature lifecycle for both hardware and software.

## Responsibilities
- **Product Lifecycle Management:** Manage the complete product/feature lifecycle of hardware and software.
- **Market Analysis:** Systematically observe and analyze the market, competition, and customer needs to derive trends, product strategy, and future product portfolios for successful market positioning.
- **Coordination & Planning:** Coordinate and prioritize software and hardware development tasks with clear requirements and forward-looking, structured planning.
- **Cross-functional Collaboration:** Serve as a central interface in close exchange with hardware and software development, UX/UI design, marketing, sales, service, logistics, purchasing, finance, the business owner, and management.
- **Enablement:** Enable marketing, sales, and service to operate successfully in the market through product expertise and convincing product documentation.
- **KPI Monitoring:** Continuously steer and monitor the product portfolio using KPIs such as revenue, results, and customer satisfaction.

## Requirements

### Education
- Completed degree (e.g., **Economics, Industrial Engineering, UX/UI Design**) or a degree in **Building/Supply Engineering** with additional business qualifications.
- A vocational training in one of the mentioned technical areas prior to studying is considered a plus.

### Personality & Work Style
- Structured, efficient, and customer-oriented way of working.
- High self-motivation, implementation-strong mentality, acting independently, entrepreneurially, and result-oriented.
- Combination of strategic thinking and practical hands-on ability.
- Strong organizational skills to maintain an overview of multiple parallel topics.
- Reliable, assertive, and a team player with a focus on customer needs.

### Experience and Know-How
- **Several years of professional experience** in operational product management for smart solutions and/or in the **HVAC or energy sector**.
- Experience in the **B2C business**.
- Practical application of knowledge in **software (ideally also hardware) development processes**.
- Methodological proficiency in market and competitive analysis and expertise in product marketing.

### Languages
- Ability to structure and present complex issues simply and vividly (verbal and written).
- Target-group and situation-specific communication skills.
- **Very good German and English skills** (written and spoken) are required.

## Benefits
- **Flexible working models:** Including various part-time options, mobile working, and job sharing.
- **Inclusive Culture:** A culture deeply anchored in diversity and inclusion.

## Contact & Location
- **HR Contact:** Emelie Schäfer (+49 711 811 7388)
- **Department Contact:** Claus Schuberth (+49 711 811 12755)
- **Location:** Schockenriedstraße 17, 70565 Stuttgart, Germany
summary: Bosch is seeking a Product Manager for their Climate & Comfort Smart Home division in Stuttgart. The role involves managing the full lifecycle of hardware and software products, conducting market analysis, and coordinating with cross-functional teams in a B2C environment.

title: PreMaster Programm - GapYear - Software Engineer CI/CD
description:
## Company Overview
At **Bosch Rexroth**, everything revolves around movement and the success of our customers. With our connectable drive and control technologies as well as digital solutions, machines and plants operate efficiently, safely and powerfully. We offer our customers components, system solutions and services for mobile and industrial applications as well as for factory automation. In all areas, we value the know-how and commitment of our employees and enable dynamic careers in an international environment.

Around 33,800 people in over 80 countries - and one corporate purpose: **"We move industries to make our planet a better place."**

## Role Overview: PreMaster Programm - GapYear
Our **PreMaster Programm** is a two-stage qualification program for committed Bachelor graduates who aim to complete a Master's degree.

- **Phase 1:** Up to **12 months of practical experience** after the Bachelor's degree to become familiar with technical and entrepreneurial contexts.
- **Phase 2:** Independent Master's studies at a university of your choice. During this time, there are further events, seminars, and personal support through a mentor on the way to graduation. Additionally, there is the opportunity to contribute to the company as a student employee during your studies.

As a participant in our PreMaster Program, you will be a member of the **Engineering Software Continuous-X** department.

## What You Will Do / Responsibilities
- Actively participate in the further development and validation of **CI/CD**.
- Work closely with experienced functional developers, simulation experts, and product managers.
- Accompany the implementation of customer-specific requirements into innovative control solutions.
- Actively shape development processes and apply modern software development methods.

## Requirements
- **Education:** Above-average completed university degree (**Bachelor**) in Informatics, Computer Science, or a comparable course of study, with the goal of pursuing a Master's degree.

- **Languages:** Very good **German** and **English** (written and spoken).

## Additional Information
- **Start Date:** By arrangement
- **Diversity and Inclusion:** Bosch Rexroth welcomes all applications regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

**Contact Persons:**
- Technical questions: Angela Schuettler (+49(9352)18-6250)
- Application process questions: Lena Steigerwald (+49(9352)18-2358)
summary: Bosch Rexroth is offering a two-phase PreMaster Program for Bachelor graduates in Computer Science. The role involves 12 months of practical experience in CI/CD within the Engineering Software Continuous-X department, followed by a supported Master's degree.

title: Promotionsstelle: Systemarchitektur für invasive Mikro-Drucksensoren
description:
## Company Information
**Bosch Sensortec GmbH** is a leading international provider of sensor solutions based on microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) for consumer electronics. They develop and market key technologies for smartphones, tablets, hearables, wearables, smartglasses, augmented and virtual reality applications, game consoles, and more. MEMS sensors are an essential part of the foundation of a connected world. Bosch Sensortec GmbH is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Robert Bosch GmbH.

## Role Overview
Bosch is offering an interesting **PhD position (Promotionsstelle)** in the field of **System Architecture for invasive micro-pressure sensors** within the medical sector. In this position, you will have the opportunity to contribute to innovative projects and develop new applications for intelligent MEMS sensors.

## Responsibilities
Your tasks include, among others:
- **System Architecture**: Research and definition of hardware and software architectures as well as interfaces for an invasive blood pressure sensor.
- **Concept and Simulation**: Development from the idea to the proof of concept using model-based simulation and verification.
- **Requirements Analysis**: Derivation of technical and medical requirements and coordination of research activities between departments.
- **Experimental Validation**: Planning and execution of experiments to analyze sensor behavior and optimize system functions.
- **Knowledge Management**: Documentation of research results, writing scientific publications, and presenting research results at conferences.

## Requirements
- **Personality**: Enjoy working in international and interdisciplinary teams; enthusiastic and creative approach.
- **Working Style**: Independent and goal-oriented work manner.
- **Experience and Know-How**: Initial experience with sensors (MEMS) and/or medical technology; sound knowledge of signal processing and modeling tools (e.g., **Matlab/Simulink**).
- **Languages**: Very good English skills.
- **Education**: Degree as M.Sc./Dipl. in Electrical Engineering, Medical Engineering, Physics, Microsystems Engineering, or a related field with very good academic performance.

## What We Offer
- An inspiring and challenging environment to successfully complete your PhD.
- Close collaboration with an interdisciplinary team.
- Access to state-of-the-art technology and resources.
- Collaboration with the Bosch site in Reutlingen (including planned travel to Reutlingen for onboarding and thereafter).
- Opportunity to participate in national and international conferences to present research results.

## Diversity and Inclusion
Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in the company culture. Applications are welcome regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

## Additional Information
- The final award of the PhD topic is subject to the criteria of the supervising university as the central contact for the doctorate.
- **Contact for Application Process**: Samuel Zinn (HR Department) +49 7121 35-33717
- **Contact for Task Description**: Bert Kaiser (Specialist Department) +49 351 8547-3799
summary: Bosch Sensortec is offering a PhD position focused on the system architecture of invasive micro-pressure sensors for medical applications. The role involves developing hardware/software architectures, conducting model-based simulations, and validating sensors experimentally in an interdisciplinary environment.
benefits: Access to state-of-the-art technology and resources
Participation in national and international conferences

title: Graduate Specialist Program (Trainee) - Technischer Vertrieb Connected Hydraulics
description:

## Application Process
- A CV and relevant certificates are sufficient; a cover letter is not required.
- Bosch Rexroth promotes diversity and inclusion regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch Rexroth is offering an 18-24 month Graduate Specialist Program for trainees in Technical Sales for Connected Hydraulics. The program involves rotations across various departments and an international assignment, leading to a role as a technical sales expert focusing on digitalization and electro-hydraulics.
benefits: Onboarding support from a peer trainee

title: Chief Expert - RADAR and ADAS Sensors - Research (SL2, w/m/div.)
description:
## Company Overview
**Bei Bosch gestalten wir Zukunft** mit hochwertigen Technologien und Dienstleistungen, die Begeisterung wecken und das Leben der Menschen verbessern. Unser Versprechen an unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter steht dabei felsenfest: Wir wachsen gemeinsam, haben Freude an unserer Arbeit und inspirieren uns gegenseitig. Willkommen bei Bosch.

## Role Overview
**Chief Expert - RADAR and ADAS Sensors - Research (SL2, w/m/div.)**

## What You Will Do / Responsibilities
- **Strategic Roadmap:** Shape the strategic research roadmap for **RADAR, Video, and USS technologies** and align them continuously with business units to ensure long-term business and technology leadership for Bosch XC-AC.
- **Trend Scouting:** Identify new technologies and innovative applications, monitor competitors and market trends (patents, trade fairs, publications, conferences), and derive recommendations for action.
- **Technical Coordination:** Responsible for the technical coordination of all RADAR, Video, and USS research activities within corporate research, including **Artificial Intelligence (AI)**, in close coordination with involved business units (XC + ME).
- **Holistic Approach:** Maintain a holistic view of products, necessary tools, and technology enablers (e.g., **Simulation, Data Management, Architectures**) to achieve optimal distribution of activities and derive systemic sensor sets and requirements.
- **External Engagement:** Actively participate in external research activities and events (e.g., **Keynote Speeches**) and initiate, participate in, or lead industry consortia (e.g., frequency allocation, interface standardization).
- **Technology Strategy:** Actively develop the technology strategy for RADAR, Video, and USS, coordinating closely with research departments and business units.
- **Technical Depth:** Develop pioneering strategies for all RADAR levels—from **high-frequency hardware** and **baseband signal processing** to **functional software**, with a strong focus on **Artificial Intelligence**.
- **Data Strategy:** Define and drive the data strategy for all **Machine Learning** topics within RADAR technology.
- **Architecture:** Responsible for the partitioning of hardware and software for **central and decentralized RADAR architectures**.
- **Sensor Set Definition:** Continuously contribute to the definition of the Bosch sensor set across all modalities (**Video, LIDAR, USS, RADAR**) for automation levels **L0 to L5** and promote cross-domain exchange to identify synergies.
- **Internal Communication:** Participate in company-wide communication instruments (newsletters, colloquiums, conference management meetings) and lead or participate in portfolio meetings or specific Radar, Video, and USS activities.
- **Expert Organization:** Work within the Bosch expert organization to continuously develop your peer group and act as a contact person for junior experts.

## Requirements

### Education
- Completed **Master's or PhD (Doctorate)** in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or Natural Sciences.

### Experience and Know-how
- Deep knowledge in **RADAR, High-Frequency Technology, Hardware Development, Signal Processing, AI**, and **data-driven development methods**.
- Experience with functions based on RADAR (**AEB, ACC, HAD**).
- Knowledge of **Video and USS sensor technology**.
- Know-how in functional contexts with other sensors (**LIDAR, VIDEO, USS**).
- Experience with partitioning in the vehicle network (**Network, Data Rates**).
- Transfer of skills to and from other technical domains (**Signal Theory, Embedded Systems, Mathematics, Data Engineering**).
- Contribution to non-technical domains such as **Marketing and Competence Development**.

### Personality and Working Style
- Ability to define a clear strategic direction for long-term business success and communicate it clearly up to the **Board level**.
- Ability to represent Bosch interests confidently in international committees and projects.
- Skillful integration of cost perspectives (product & development) and technical requirements.
- Determination to successfully drive disruptive technologies forward.
- Ability to motivate others and promote constructive teamwork.

### Enthusiasm
- Passion for maintaining internal and external Bosch networks and contributing to the development of the Bosch expert organization.

### Languages
- Very good **English**.

## Benefits
- Flexible working models (various part-time options, mobile working, job sharing).
- Inclusive corporate culture regarding gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch is seeking a Chief Expert to lead the strategic research roadmap for RADAR, Video, and USS sensor technologies. The role focuses on integrating AI and Machine Learning into sensor architectures to maintain technological leadership in automotive automation levels L0-L5.

## Role Overview
As an **AI Research Engineer at Bosch**, you will explore a variety of real-world use cases that can significantly benefit from cutting-edge Large Language Models (LLMs), end-to-end trained ADAS systems, and AI agents.

## What You Will Do
- Develop efficient **engineering and MLOps solutions** to tailor models to specific Bosch applications (e.g., fine-tuning generic language models using proprietary data for industrial applications).
- Collaborate with corporate research departments and business units to identify and develop novel applications of AI across Bosch's product and service portfolio.
- Use an **entrepreneurial mindset** to evaluate, develop, and pitch new ideas to drive innovation.
- Generate and secure **intellectual property** through high-quality publications in leading AI journals and conferences.
- Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code supporting both research and technology transfer.
- Assess emerging machine learning technologies to provide strategic insights and influence Bosch's technological development.
- Work within a dynamic AI department alongside ML experts and global specialists to maximize business impact.
- Conduct experiments on **on-premises GPU clusters** and **cloud infrastructure**.

## Requirements

### Education
- Excellent **MSc in Computer Science** or related fields.
- **PhD in Machine Learning** with a focus on Large Language Models, generative AI, or foundation models.

### Experience and Knowledge
- Proven research track record and multiple years of hands-on expertise in **LLMs, GenAI, agentic AI, end-2-end automotive models, or vision-language models**.

title: Master Thesis Data-Efficient Hybrid Machine Learning for Robust Vibration System Prediction
description:
## Role Overview
At **Bosch**, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: we grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Join in and feel the difference.

Robert Bosch GmbH is looking for a student to work on a Master Thesis focusing on **Data-Efficient Hybrid Machine Learning for Robust Vibration System Prediction**. In collaboration with a team of engineers and scientists, you will investigate how to develop more robust and reliable predictive models for technical systems, enhancing a machine-learning toolbox to forecast vibration-loaded systems, specifically when measurement data is scarce.

## What You Will Do
- Research and apply **advanced machine learning techniques** to integrate limited measurement data into the training of models that currently rely predominantly on simulation data.
- Develop a benchmark by integrating simulated data and new measurement data from a test bench, utilizing **machine learning algorithms** to predict the dynamic behavior of **nonlinear coupled vibration systems**.
- Apply and evaluate chosen approaches, comparing model performance (**accuracy and robustness**) against simulation-only trained models.
- Openly communicate ideas and contributions, benefiting from exchange with colleagues, experts, and a broader network across various domains and locations within the company.

## Requirements

### Education
- **Master studies** in the field of **Engineering, Mathematics, Physics** or comparable with good grades.
- Must be currently enrolled at a university.

### Experience and Knowledge
- Good understanding of **dynamics (mechanical vibrations) / mechanics**.
- Very good knowledge of **Python** (including **Pytorch, Pandas, Numpy**, etc.).
- Good to very good knowledge of **fundamental machine learning concepts and algorithms**, particularly relevant for **regression**.

### Personality and Working Practice
- High degree of **self-motivation** to drive innovation.
- Ability to work **independently** while communicating progress and ideas effectively.

### Work Routine
- **On-site presence** is required.

### Languages
- Fluent in **English** and basic in **German** OR fluent in **German** and very good in **English**.

## Additional Information
- **Start:** According to prior agreement
- **Duration:** 6 months
- **Application Documents:** CV, transcript of records, examination regulations, and if indicated, a valid work and residence permit.

## Company Culture
Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in our corporate culture. We welcome all applications, regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch is seeking a Master's student for a 6-month thesis project focused on developing data-efficient hybrid machine learning models to predict the behavior of nonlinear coupled vibration systems. The role involves integrating limited real-world measurement data with simulation data using Python and PyTorch.
benefits: Collaborative environment with engineers and scientists
Access to a broad network of experts across various domains and locations

title: Instandhalter (w/m/div.)
description:
## Role Overview

Die **Koller und Schwemmer GmbH** (Röthensteig 21, 90408 Nürnberg) ist ein mittelständisches Unternehmen und eine Tochtergesellschaft der Bosch-Gruppe. Mit dieser Identifikation gilt das Unternehmen als flexibler Problemlöser rund um die Diesel-Einspritztechnologie.

description:

Bosch is on a mission to harness the power of large-scale time-series data to optimize industrial processes and create innovative solutions. We are building the next generation of foundation models for time-series to be utilized across the various fields of the Bosch group, such as manufacturing, mobility solutions, energy systems, and IoT applications.

As a **Research Engineer**, you will focus on the efficient implementation of scalable architectures for large-scale time-series models. You will help push the state-of-the-art in model scalability by contributing to the core systems for our products.

## What You Will Do
- **Optimize training and inference pipelines**, enhancing their performance in terms of runtime and memory usage.
- Apply and refine scaled models for a diverse array of industrial use cases, leveraging massive datasets from Bosch's proprietary sources to solve real-world challenges.
- Design and optimize training and inference pipelines for time-series foundation models.
- Develop efficient algorithms for processing and analyzing massive industrial datasets with foresight and precision.
- Research and develop scalable architectures for large-scale time-series modeling in an industrial environment to set new standards.
- Drive continuous performance optimization and benchmarking of time-series models to ensure maximum efficiency.
- Implement and deploy scalable solutions for industrial time-series applications in close collaboration with engineering teams and business units.

## Requirements

### Experience and Knowledge
- Demonstrated proficiency in **scalable machine learning architectures** and **distributed training** (including multi-GPU/multi-node training).

- Proven ability to translate theoretical methods into practical, high-quality code for scalable machine learning.
- Profound experience in **large-scale data processing** in an industrial environment.
- Excellent knowledge of **MLOps** (e.g., CI/CD pipelines, experiment tracking, containerization) in an industrial setting.

description:

Bosch Service Solutions GmbH is looking forward to your application!

As a **Senior HW/SW Systems Engineer**, you are the driving force behind the development of central HW/SW components of our end-to-end **Stolen Vehicle Tracking solution** and their implementation in international customer projects. In addition to development, you take responsibility for certification and accreditation processes on an international level. Your expertise in telematics-based solutions and your ability to translate existing regulations into technical requirements are crucial for the success of this strategically important service.

## What You Will Do / Responsibilities

- Manage the development of our **Stolen Vehicle Tracking Hardware** from conception to series production.
- Responsible for the planning, monitoring, and execution of all necessary **accreditations and HW/SW certifications** (e.g., **Thatcham, SCM, UNECE, CE, RED, ECE R10, FCC**) in Europe and beyond.
- Coordinate testing laboratories, support tests through debugging, and ensure hardware and firmware compliance.
- Continuously monitor regulatory requirements and industry standards in the Stolen Vehicle Services and telematics environment to proactively ensure compliance.
- Advise the HW/SW development process and create/review relevant documentation (**test plans, reports, technical documents, declarations of conformity, installation guidelines**).
- Ensure product suitability for permanent vehicle installation and create corresponding installation guidelines.
- Perform **risk analyses** for hardware revisions and firmware updates and define the certification strategy for product changes.
- Support audits and technical customer tests.

- **German:** Fluent (mother tongue).

title: IT Product Security Specialist (m/w/div.)
description:
## Role Overview

**Möchten Sie Ihre Ideen in nutzbringende und sinnvolle Technologien verwandeln?** Ob im Bereich Mobility Solutions, Consumer Goods, Industrial Technology oder Energy and Building Technology – mit uns verbessern Sie die Lebensqualität der Menschen auf der ganzen Welt. Willkommen bei Bosch.

Die Bosch Service Solutions GmbH freut sich auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Werde Teil unseres Teams als **IT Product Security Specialist** und leiste einen bedeutenden Beitrag, indem du die Sicherheit unserer vielfältigen IT‑Landschaft – von lokalen Rechenzentren bis hin zu Multi‑Cloud‑Plattformen – konzipierst und verantwortest.

## Responsibilities

- **Lead the Product Security Program**: Integrate security into the SDLC, perform Threat Modeling, and derive security requirements for features and architecture changes.
- **Risk Analysis**: Conduct risk analyses and prioritize product-related risks (Design, dependencies, compliance).
- **Product Roadmap Integration**: Work closely with Product Managers to integrate security into product roadmaps and planning.
- **Governance Liaison**: Act as an intermediary between governance and technical implementation; define security guidelines and actively participate in their implementation and enforcement across various products.
- **Tooling & Automation**: Develop and implement secure tooling and automation processes in CI/CD pipelines (SCA, SAST/DAST, Secret Scanning).
- **Vulnerability Management**: Manage Vulnerability Triage and Remediation SLAs and participate in Incident Response for product security incidents (Root Cause Analysis, customer communication, long-term measures).
- **Compliance Monitoring**: Monitor new regulatory requirements (e.g., EU Cyber Resilience Act, NIS2, Cybersecurity Labeling) and ensure compliance.
- **Enablement**: Create and conduct security trainings, guidelines, and enablement initiatives for engineering and product teams.
- **Reporting**: Report relevant security metrics and program status to management.

## Requirements

### Professional Experience
- **5–10 years** of experience in Product Security, Application Security, or roles with a focus on Secure SDLC.

### Technical Know-how
- Solid technical background in **Software Development, Security Engineering, or Architecture**.
- Comprehensive knowledge of threats and countermeasures (**OWASP**, Secure Design Patterns, authentication models, supply chain risks).
- Experience with **Cloud Security** or relevant standards/regulations (e.g., **NIST SSDF, ISO 21434**).
- Practical experience in **Threat Modeling (STRIDE** or similar) and the use of risk assessment frameworks.
- Proficiency with development tools as well as **agile and DevOps** environments.

### Skills
- Strong stakeholder communication across all levels to ensure clear alignment and effective collaboration.

### Personal Characteristics
- High self-motivation and independence.
- Strong sense of responsibility.
- Proactive way of working.

### Certifications
- **CISM** or **CISSP** are an advantage, but not mandatory.

### Languages
- Fluent **German** and **English** skills (written and spoken).

## Contact Persons
- **Department**: Mr. Andreas Thiele
- **HR**: Ms. Simone Hotz
summary: Bosch is seeking an IT Product Security Specialist to lead their Product Security Program, focusing on integrating security into the SDLC, threat modeling, and ensuring compliance with regulations like NIS2. The role involves managing CI/CD security tooling and coordinating between governance and technical execution across multi-cloud and on-prem environments.

description:
## Role Overview

In this key role, you are the central interface between our business unit and the Bosch corporate headquarters. You ensure that our products comply with all relevant national and international compliance requirements from conception to market launch and beyond.

## Responsibilities
- Responsible for **Product Compliance**, **Product Engineering**, and **Product Cybersecurity** within Bosch SO.

- Act as the primary contact for product compliance topics between the business unit and the Bosch corporate headquarters, coordinating strategies and ensuring consistent implementation of group-wide guidelines.

- Develop and implement product engineering and compliance processes and tools within the business unit; responsible for the further development of the **SOCD09010 (Product Engineering Process)** in coordination with other central functions (e.g., QMM, PM, UX).
- Identify potential compliance risks in product development, production, and sales and develop risk mitigation strategies.
- Train business unit employees on relevant compliance topics and promote high awareness of product safety and conformity.

## Requirements

### Education
- Successfully completed degree in **Engineering, Law, Industrial Engineering**, or a comparable qualification.

### Professional Experience
- Several years of relevant professional experience in product development, product certification, or quality assurance, ideally in an international environment.

### Know-how
- Sound knowledge of relevant national and international laws and regulations (e.g., **Product Safety Act, CE directives, REACH, RoHS, WEEE**, etc.).
- Specific knowledge of relevant standards/laws for the business area (e.g., **Automotive standards, IEC 61508, MedGV**) is an advantage.

### Skills
- Strong communication and moderation skills to convey complex issues clearly and target-group specifically.
- Ability to interact successfully with various stakeholders internally and with corporate headquarters.
- High analytical skills and the ability to quickly grasp complex issues and act solution-oriented.

### Personal Characteristics
- Initiative and sense of responsibility.
- Independent, structured, and result-oriented way of working.

### Languages
- **Business fluent German and English** (written and spoken) are mandatory.
- Additional language skills are an advantage.

### Other
- Occasional willingness to travel (national and international) is required.

## Contact
HR: Ms. Simone Hotz
summary: Bosch is seeking a Product Engineering and Compliance Officer to act as the primary link between their business unit and corporate headquarters. title: Master Thesis GPU-Accelerated Particle Simulation for OpenFOAM
description:
## Role Overview
At Bosch, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: we grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Join in and feel the difference.

In many industrial and research applications, tracking millions of particles within a fluid flow is essential, but current CPU-based methods are too slow. This project offers an exciting opportunity to break that bottleneck by building a state-of-the-art solver that leverages the massive power of modern GPUs to accelerate simulations by orders of magnitude. Join us in developing a cutting-edge tool that integrates directly with the industry-standard Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) software OpenFOAM, and make a significant impact on what's possible in computational science.

## What You Will Do
As a part of your Master thesis, you will:
- Conduct a **literature review** on state-of-the-art methods for Lagrangian Particle Tracking, GPU computing in CFD and particle-wall interaction.
- Develop a **high-performance particle tracking solver** using Python and the NVIDIA Warp framework to run on the GPU.
- Implement a method for **collision detection** with complex 3D geometries and create a **pre-processing pipeline** to prepare simulation data (e.g., flow fields and meshes) from OpenFOAM for the GPU.
- Integrate the new solver into a standard **OpenFOAM workflow**, establishing a seamless data exchange.
- Validate and **benchmark** your solver's performance against OpenFOAM's native tools, quantifying the massive speedup.

## Requirements

### Education
- Master studies in the field of **Engineering, Physics or comparable**.

### Experience and Knowledge
- Profound knowledge of and experience in **numerical simulation methods**.
- Solid understanding of **continuum mechanics**.
- Very good programming skills in **Python**.
- Initial experience with **OpenFOAM, NVIDIA Warp and ParaView** is desirable.
- Ideally, initial experience with **Git** and working on **HPC clusters**.

### Personality and Working Practice
- Able to proactively develop new ideas, find solutions independently and complete tasks efficiently.
- Demonstrating a high degree of self-motivation.

### Languages
- **English**: Business fluent.
- **German**: Good.

## Additional Information
- **Work Routine**: Partially mobile working possible, ideally with 3 - 4 office days per week.
- **Start**: According to prior agreement.
- **Duration**: 6 months.
- **Mandatory Requirement**: Enrollment at university. Please attach CV, transcript of records, examination regulations and (if indicated) a valid work and residence permit.

## Company Culture
Diversity and inclusion are not just trends for us but are firmly anchored in our corporate culture. Therefore, we welcome all applications, regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin or sexual identity.

## Contact
Alexander Fuchs (Functional Department)
+49 711 811 93399
summary: Bosch is seeking a Master's student for a 6-month thesis project to develop a GPU-accelerated particle tracking solver for OpenFOAM. The role involves using Python and NVIDIA Warp to improve simulation speed and implementing collision detection for complex 3D geometries.

title: PhD Thesis - Scalable and explainable End2End Driving Model (SEED)
description:
## Role Overview

Your goal is to be the architect of a next-generation, modular, and scalable end-to-end driving model (SEED), exploring the balance between data-driven power and safety-critical explainability. This position offers a bridge between academic excellence and industrial application, allowing you to publish research at top-tier venues while seeing contributions integrated into real-world ADAS products.

## Your Contribution to the Future of Driving
- **Master the State-of-the-Art:** Conduct a deep-dive analysis of current breakthroughs in end-to-end autonomous driving and investigate how to exploit large-scale datasets without relying on labeled data for every sub-task.
- **Innovate and Develop:** Design and implement novel deep neural network architectures that prioritize both high-performance scaling and human-interpretable explainability.
- **Experiment and Validate:** Validate the performance and reliability of your models through rigorous experiments on public benchmarks and massive, Bosch-owned autonomous driving datasets.
- **From Lab to Road:** Collaborate with expert project teams in deep learning and computer vision to brainstorm new ideas and deploy software on hardware, validating research on real automotive driving systems.
- **Share Your Success:** Publish findings and research outcomes at top-tier computer vision and machine learning journals and conferences.

## Requirements
- **Education:** Master degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science or similar with excellent grades.
- **Experience and Knowledge:** Detailed knowledge of **computer vision**, **3D vision**, **machine learning**, **deep learning**, **artificial intelligence** (Transformer, Sparse and BEV Queries) and experience with dedicated development tools (**TensorFlow**, **PyTorch**, **Python**).
- **Personality and Working Practice:** High level of enthusiasm, creativity, ability to work independently with excellent task-management, team spirit, assertiveness, and persuasiveness.
- **Enthusiasm:** Interest in conceptual work and method-based design.
- **Languages:** Fluent in English and German written and spoken.

- **Application:** Please submit CV, certificates, and links to GitHub or Kaggle accounts.
- **Diversity:** Bosch welcomes all applications regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

## Role Overview
Join a team of over 3,600 employees across more than 50 locations in Germany. As consultants, installers, and service providers, the team develops and implements networked solutions in building security, building automation, and energy efficiency based on the latest technologies.

## Requirements
- Sound knowledge in the development of **APIs**, specifically **REST** and/or **GraphQL**.
- Proven experience in developing applications in **cloud environments** such as **Kubernetes**, **Microsoft Azure**, **GCP**, and/or **AWS**.
- Proficiency with **SOLID principles** and **Clean Code** practices.
- In-depth understanding and application of **Software Design Patterns**.
- Strong architectural understanding and the ability to conceptualize complex software solutions.
- Sound knowledge of at least one modern **Java framework** such as **Spring** (especially **Spring Boot**), **Quarkus**, and/or **Micronaut**.
- Experience with modularization concepts such as **Microservices**, **Modulith**, or **Function-as-a-Service (FaaS)**.
- Experienced use of test automation tools such as **JUnit** and **Mockito**.
- Familiarity with modern, **Azure-based services**, event-driven software designs, and toolkits for the entire software development lifecycle.

## Nice-to-Haves / Additional Qualifications
- Experience with **NoSQL databases**.
- Practical experience in **CI/CD** (e.g., **Jenkins**, **GitLab CI**, **Azure DevOps**).
- Knowledge of **containerization** (e.g., **Docker**, **Podman**).
- Experience with **time-series databases** and their processing.
- Familiarity with monitoring tools like **Grafana**.
- Initial experience with **Infrastructure as Code (IaC)** tools such as **Pulumi**.
- Interest and/or experience in the field of **IoT**.

Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in the corporate culture. Applications are welcomed regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

title: Data Platform Engineer (Mid-Level) (m/f/d)
description:
We’re looking for a **Data Platform Engineer (m/f/d)** to join our team and help build and operate the data infrastructure that powers Flix’s mobility platform.

At FlixTech, our data platform enables teams across the company to process, analyze, and act on large volumes of mobility data. The platform supports critical use cases such as network planning, pricing, operations, and business analytics. You will work on scalable infrastructure that processes millions of daily events and ensures reliable data availability across the organization.

You’ll be part of a cross-functional engineering environment collaborating with data analysts, data scientists, and platform engineers. The role focuses on building reliable, scalable data systems and enabling other teams to work efficiently with data.

## About the Role
- Design, build, and maintain scalable data infrastructure running on **AWS** and **Kubernetes**
- Develop and operate streaming and data platform components using technologies such as **Kafka** and **Snowflake**
- Implement monitoring, alerting, and operational tooling to ensure platform reliability and performance
- Own features end-to-end, including development, testing, deployment, monitoring, and maintenance
- Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code using **Terraform** and **GitLab CI/CD**
- Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and stakeholders to support data platform use cases
- Contribute to improving platform reliability, automation, and engineering practices
- Participate in on-call rotations to support the stability of the data platform

## About You
- **3+ years of experience** building or operating data infrastructure or data platforms
- Hands-on experience with streaming technologies such as **Kafka** and data warehouses like **Snowflake**
- Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools (e.g., **Terraform**) and CI/CD pipelines (e.g., **GitLab**)
- Practical experience with cloud platforms (preferably **AWS**) and container platforms such as **Kubernetes**
- Experience with monitoring and observability tools (e.g., **Datadog** or similar)
- Solid programming skills in **Python** and interest in writing clean, maintainable code
- Strong collaboration and communication skills when working with technical and business stakeholders
- Fluent in **English**

### Nice to have:
- Experience with advanced **SQL** or data transformation tools (e.g., **dbt**)
- Experience with data processing frameworks (e.g., **PySpark**)
- Knowledge of security and networking concepts relevant for data platforms

We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you!

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
- **Work from (M)Anywhere**: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
- **Hybrid work model**: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
- **Wellbeing support**: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
- **Learning & Development**: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
- **Mentoring Program**: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.

## Why Join Flix?
At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

title: Kotlin Backend Engineer (m/f/d)
description:
We’re looking for a talented mid-level **Kotlin Engineer (m/f/d)** to join us in Berlin or Munich.

In the Foundation Division of FlixTech, our Finance Technology Stream helps the business grow efficiently by delivering smart, compliant, and automated finance solutions. We keep things simple for our internal users so they can spend less time on manual tasks and more time on what matters. The team you will join is not focused on user-facing experiences or polished UIs. Our success is measured by data correctness, consistency, and trustworthiness, even in complex and evolving business scenarios. You’ll be part of a self-managed, cross-functional team with a Product Owner and fellow Software Engineers, directly supporting new country launches, new business models, and the continuous evolution of Flix’s financial backbone.

This role is well-suited for someone who enjoys ownership, working close to real business problems, and contributing to the long-term foundations of an international company. If you value collaboration, thoughtful engineering, and building systems that need to be right, not just fast, we’d love to meet you.

## About the Role
- Design, develop, and maintain **backend services** using **Kotlin**
- Build and evolve **scalable, event-driven systems** that process, validate, and expose financial data
- Own features end-to-end: from **technical design and implementation** to **testing, deployment, monitoring, and ongoing maintenance**
- Collaborate closely with the Product Owner and Finance stakeholders to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions
- Ensure **data accuracy, traceability, and auditability** across financial and accounting flows
- Improve system reliability, observability, and performance for business-critical processes
- Contribute to **clean code, architectural discussions, and continuous improvement** of engineering practices

## About You
- **3 years of experience** as a Software Engineer working with **Kotlin**
- Hands-on experience building backend services using the **Spring ecosystem** (e.g. **Spring Boot, Spring Data**)
- Experience with **event-driven design** (e.g. asynchronous processing, messaging, domain events)
- Practical experience with **cloud platforms** (preferably **AWS**, or similar)
- Solid understanding of **databases**, both **NoSQL** (e.g. **DynamoDB**) and/or relational systems
- Comfortable working in **complex, data-heavy domains** (ideally Finance), where investigation, analysis, and precision are key
- Communicate clearly in **English**, open-minded, curious, and motivated to continuously improve both yourself and the systems you work on

We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you!

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
- **Work from (M)Anywhere**: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
- **Hybrid work model**: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
- **Wellbeing support**: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
- **Learning & Development**: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
- **Mentoring Program**: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.

To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer (m/f/d) - Station Management & Inventory
description:
## Senior Full-Stack Engineer (m/f/d) - Station Management & Inventory

We are looking for a motivated and curious **Senior Full-Stack Engineer (Java/Kotlin)** to join our team in Supply Division and drive customer station experience to the next level!

In an ever growing network, Flix customers worldwide expect a comfortable and seamless experience at our stations as part of their travel experience. Our self-organized, cross-functional and distributed team builds products to enable Flix station managers to scale management of the station inventory, which a wide range of internal tech teams across Flix depend on, and overall serves to improve the experience at our stations for millions of passengers.

In this role, you will continue to shape the product by driving innovation, solving complex challenges, and creating a lasting impact on our organization.

## About the Role
- You take ownership of roadmap epics as topic driver, aligning technical requirements and solutions across the team together with the product owner
- As part of the team you will work closely with business stakeholders on continuously developing and improving the product vision
- You always keep the business value in mind when making decisions
- You drive the development process using an agile environment
- As part of your daily work, you collaborate closely with your fellow team members, for example in pair and ensemble programming, doing code reviews, testing, and operations
- You frequently deliver new versions of the product, using continuous integration and delivery
- You work confidently with or learn how to use cutting-edge technologies and tools including but not limited to:
    - **Kotlin** across server and browser
    - **JVM** on server-side (**Ktor**, **Axon Framework**)
    - Component-based Frontend (**Compose HTML**, or similar libraries and frameworks)
    - Distributed system architecture (**Apache Kafka**, **HTTP/REST**)
    - Infrastructure with **CI/CD** (**AWS**, **Docker**, **Kubernetes**, **Terraform**, **Datadog**, **Gitlab**)

## About You
- **5+ years of software engineering experience**, primarily backend development with a JVM language
- Willingness to work with **Kotlin**
- Solid front-end skills with modern front-end stack
